checks.describe: the Compose model service is DOWN — one heavyweight stack at a time (Step 1). The KIND phase must not run alongside the Compose model server. The registry compose service may stay up (it is the model source)
checks.run: if curl -fsS --max-time 2 http://localhost:8080/health >/dev/null 2>&1 && ! curl -fsS --max-time 2 http://localhost:5100/v2/_catalog >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo compose-model-up; else echo compose-model-down; fi

checks.describe: the slim single-node KIND cluster is up and its node reports Ready, reached through the ISOLATED kubeconfig (Step 1). Self-seeds by running make k8s-up if the cluster is absent
checks.run: export KUBECONFIG=labs/opsmate/k8s/kubeconfig; kubectl get nodes >/dev/null 2>&1 || make -C labs/opsmate k8s-up >/dev/null 2>&1; kubectl get nodes 2>/dev/null | grep -c ' Ready ' | xargs -I{} sh -c 'if [ {} -ge 1 ]; then echo node-ready; else echo node-not-ready; fi'

checks.describe: the cluster runs with an isolated kubeconfig at labs/opsmate/k8s/kubeconfig naming the throwaway cluster — the visitor-badge discipline, the default context untouched (Step 1)
checks.run: F=labs/opsmate/k8s/kubeconfig; if [ -s "$F" ] && grep -q 'opsmate' "$F"; then echo kubeconfig-isolated; else echo kubeconfig-missing; fi

checks.describe: the M7 registry is up and the signed opsmate/model:1.0.0 is present — the model source the cluster pulls from (Step 3, the M7 seam). Self-seeds the registry + push via the M7 seed if reachable
checks.run: bash labs/m7/seed.sh >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; T=$(curl -fsS --max-time 5 http://localhost:5100/v2/opsmate/model/tags/list 2>/dev/null); if echo "$T" | grep -q '"1.0.0"'; then echo kit-present; else echo kit-missing; fi

checks.describe: the capacity worksheet exists with its three derive-and-fill fields (Step 2) — values are the learner's own, so presence not numbers is asserted
checks.run: F=labs/m8/capacity-worksheet.md; if [ -s "$F" ] && grep -q 'requests.memory' "$F" && grep -q 'limits.memory' "$F" && grep -q 'replicas' "$F"; then echo worksheet-present; else echo worksheet-missing; fi

checks.describe: model-server.yaml encodes the artifact chain: a PVC, an initContainer that kit-unpacks the model layer from the registry, the llama-server container, and hostAliases for the two-paths fix (Step 4)
checks.run: F=labs/opsmate/k8s/model-server.yaml; if grep -q 'kind: PersistentVolumeClaim' "$F" && grep -q 'initContainers' "$F" && grep -q 'kit unpack' "$F" && grep -q -- '--filter=model' "$F" && grep -q 'ghcr.io/ggml-org/llama.cpp:server' "$F" && grep -q 'hostAliases' "$F"; then echo chain-ok; else echo chain-bad; fi

checks.describe: model-server.yaml has probes shaped for a slow starter: a startupProbe (the load budget) plus readiness and a lenient liveness — the sound-check vs show-must-go-on design (Step 4)
checks.run: F=labs/opsmate/k8s/model-server.yaml; if grep -q 'startupProbe' "$F" && grep -q 'readinessProbe' "$F" && grep -q 'livenessProbe' "$F"; then echo probes-ok; else echo probes-missing; fi

checks.describe: the model pod is 1/1 Running from the signed kit — the initContainer pulled the model onto the PVC and llama-server loaded it (Step 4). Self-seeds: cluster up, kits present, kubectl apply, wait Ready
checks.run: export KUBECONFIG=labs/opsmate/k8s/kubeconfig; kubectl get nodes >/dev/null 2>&1 || make -C labs/opsmate k8s-up >/dev/null 2>&1; bash labs/m7/seed.sh >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; kubectl get deploy opsmate-model >/dev/null 2>&1 || kubectl apply -f labs/opsmate/k8s/model-server.yaml >/dev/null 2>&1; kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready pod -l app=opsmate-model --timeout=180s >/dev/null 2>&1; kubectl get pods -l app=opsmate-model 2>/dev/null | grep -c '1/1 *Running' | xargs -I{} sh -c 'if [ {} -ge 1 ]; then echo pod-running; else echo pod-not-running; fi'

checks.describe: the ClusterIP Service fronts the model and answers /v1/chat/completions with a usage block, reached via a background port-forward — the completion through the cluster with the unchanged M3 client contract (Step 5)
checks.run: export KUBECONFIG=labs/opsmate/k8s/kubeconfig; kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready pod -l app=opsmate-model --timeout=180s >/dev/null 2>&1; ( kubectl port-forward service/opsmate-model 18080:8080 >/dev/null 2>&1 & echo $! > /tmp/m8-pf.pid ); sleep 4; OUT=$(curl -s --max-time 60 http://127.0.0.1:18080/v1/chat/completions -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"model":"qwen3-0.6b","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"ping"}]}' | jq -r 'if .usage.total_tokens then "chat-ok" else "chat-fail" end' 2>/dev/null); kill "$(cat /tmp/m8-pf.pid 2>/dev/null)" 2>/dev/null; rm -f /tmp/m8-pf.pid; echo "$OUT"

checks.describe: the deliberately-wrong probe patch exists for the restart-loop demo — no startupProbe plus an aggressive liveness (Step 6). The exhibit that shows the self-inflicted CrashLoopBackOff
checks.run: F=labs/opsmate/k8s/bad-liveness.yaml; if [ -s "$F" ] && grep -q 'startupProbe: null' "$F" && grep -q 'failureThreshold: 1' "$F"; then echo bad-liveness-present; else echo bad-liveness-missing; fi

checks.describe: the vLLM variant ships and is the same shape as the llama-server manifest — same PVC/initContainer/probes, differing only in image and args (Step 9). The engine fork as a two-file diff
checks.run: F=labs/opsmate/k8s/model-server-vllm.yaml; if grep -q 'vllm/vllm-openai' "$F" && grep -q 'initContainers' "$F" && grep -q 'startupProbe' "$F" && grep -q -- '--served-model-name' "$F"; then echo vllm-ok; else echo vllm-missing; fi
